title: rubus horridus var. koehleri
intro: Rubus horridus var. koehleri, also known as the wild raspberry or thorny raspberry, is a type of bramble native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and anthocyanins in its leaves and fruits. The health benefits of Rubus horridus var. koehleri include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to protect against certain types of cancer and neurodegenerative diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its fruits can be consumed fresh or used in jams and preserves, although it's essential to note that the plant can be toxic in large quantities due to its high levels of oxalic acid.
